skill.name: git-guardrails-claude-code
skill.description: Set up Claude Code hooks that block selected destructive git commands before Bash tools run them.
skill.summary: Install Claude Code git guardrails that stop risky push, reset, clean, branch deletion, and restore commands.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many shell, command, and hidden-file patterns, but most are markdown examples or intentional Claude Code hook configuration. The confirmed risk is operational: this skill installs an executable Bash hook into project or global Claude Code settings. No evidence found of network access, credential access, data exfiltration, destructive execution, or prompt injection.

medium_findings.title: Installs executable Claude Code Bash hook
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to copy a shell script into Claude Code hook directories and configure PreToolUse Bash hooks to run it. This is a legitimate guardrail design, but it changes local automation behavior and executes a shell script before Bash tool use.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hook installation and executable script are directly documented. The script content is simple and defensive, so the risk is operational rather than malicious.

medium_findings.title: Global installation can affect all Claude Code projects
medium_findings.description: The skill offers a global installation path under the user's Claude Code home configuration. If selected, the hook affects Bash tool use across projects, which is broader than a single repository.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The global settings path is explicit and the scope is described by the skill. No evidence suggests malicious global persistence, but the behavior has broad user impact.

low_findings.title: Static command execution findings are mostly false positives
low_findings.description: The markdown backtick findings are examples of git commands to block or a verification command for the user. The shell script reads JSON input and pattern matches command text; it does not execute the requested git command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The static patterns are present, but context shows they are documentation or non-executing parsing and matching. I found no shell eval, command interpolation into execution, or outbound behavior.

low_findings.title: Hidden file access is intentional Claude Code configuration
low_findings.description: The hidden paths point to .claude settings and hook directories used by Claude Code. This is expected for a hook installation skill, but users should choose project scope when they do not want global changes.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All hidden paths are visible installation targets for Claude Code settings. No hidden path is used for credential collection, concealment, or unrelated persistence.

dangerous_patterns.title: PreToolUse command hook execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The settings snippets register a command hook that runs a shell script for Bash tool use. Command hooks are powerful and should be reviewed before installation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The hook command entries are explicit in the settings examples. This is powerful but aligned with the skill's stated purpose.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell script parses requested Bash commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The hook script reads tool input and extracts the requested command with jq before matching it with grep. This does not execute the command, but it observes Bash command text.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The parser and grep check are directly visible in the script. The lack of network calls or eval reduces this from a high-risk pattern to a reviewable local hook behavior.

content.user_title: Install Git Guardrails for Claude Code
content.value_statement: AI coding sessions can run destructive git commands by mistake. This skill installs Claude Code hooks that block selected risky git operations before they execute.
actual_capabilities: Guides installation of a Claude Code PreToolUse hook for Bash commands.
Provides a shell script that blocks configured dangerous git command patterns.
Supports global installation under the user's Claude Code settings.
Includes a verification command that should return a blocked result.
Explains how to customize the blocked command pattern list.
limitations: Requires Claude Code hook support and a Bash environment.
Requires jq to parse hook input in the bundled script.
Blocks commands by regular expression patterns, not by a full git parser.
Does not protect git operations performed outside the configured Claude Code hook.

use_cases.title: Protect an active repository
use_cases.description: Install the hook only in one project to prevent accidental push, hard reset, clean, or branch deletion during AI coding work.

use_cases.title: Standardize AI coding safeguards
use_cases.description: Use the global setup path to apply the same git safety rules across several Claude Code projects.

use_cases.title: Teach safe automation boundaries
use_cases.description: Review the blocked pattern list with a team and adjust it for local workflow rules before enabling the hook.

prompt_templates.title: Install Project Guardrails
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up git guardrails for this project only. Ask before changing files, then install and verify the Claude Code hook.

prompt_templates.title: Install Global Guardrails
prompt_templates.prompt: Install the git guardrails globally for Claude Code. Show the settings changes and run the verification test afterward.

prompt_templates.title: Customize Blocked Commands
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the blocked git command patterns with me. Add any missing risky commands before installing the hook.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Existing Hook Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Inspect my existing Claude Code hook configuration and merge the git guardrails without overwriting other hooks.

output: The assistant asks whether to use project or global scope.
It copies the hook script into the selected Claude Code hook directory.
It updates the settings file and confirms that a test git push command is blocked.

output_examples.input: Add protection against hard resets and forced pushes.
output: The assistant reviews the existing blocked pattern list.
It confirms that hard reset and forced push patterns are covered.
It offers to add any team-specific command patterns before verification.

best_practices: Use project-level installation unless every Claude Code project needs the same policy.
Review the shell script and settings diff before enabling the hook.
Run the verification command after any change to blocked patterns.
anti_patterns: Do not overwrite an existing Claude Code settings file.
Do not install global hooks without telling the user the scope.
Do not rely on this hook for git commands run outside Claude Code.

faq.question: What commands does this skill block?
faq.answer: It blocks selected risky git commands, including push, reset --hard, clean, branch deletion, checkout dot, and restore dot patterns.

faq.question: Does it run git commands itself?
faq.answer: No. The bundled script reads the requested command and blocks matches before Claude Code runs the Bash tool.

faq.question: Can I install it for one project only?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill supports project-level setup under the repository's .claude directory.

faq.question: Can I install it globally?
faq.answer: Yes. Global setup uses the user's Claude Code settings and applies across projects that use that configuration.

faq.question: Can the blocked list be changed?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill instructs the assistant to ask about customization and edit the copied script when needed.

faq.question: What dependencies does the hook need?
faq.answer: The script expects Bash and jq. It also uses grep for pattern matching.
